preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Ssas trial user for an ai-backed design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation anchors the page session. For a Mover at the decision stage, the minimal variant strips distracting nav links and keeps only the logo and a single persistent 'Start Free Trial' CTA — the highest-converting element on most SaaS pages, ensuring the action is always reachable.

sections.name: Hero — Single Message, Single Action
sections.rationale: The Mover archetype at the decision stage needs zero friction above the fold: one clear headline, one primary CTA. HeroPrimary's Mover variant suppresses the subhead and secondary CTA, delivering a singular conversion-oriented message. The with-social-proof sub-variant can place a silent LogoStrip directly below the CTA cluster to reinforce momentum without adding cognitive load.

sections.name: Silent Credibility Bar
sections.rationale: Immediately after the hero, a logos-only SocialProofBar provides silent credibility — critical for a Mover at the decision stage who doesn't want to read testimonials but needs a fast trust signal that recognizable brands already use the product. The Mover variant strips the headline; logos do the talking.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics
sections.rationale: StatBlock's Mover variant delivers pure metric impact — no sources, no headline, just bold numbers (e.g., '3× faster design handoff', '60% fewer revisions'). At the decision stage, a Mover needs quantified proof of value that scans in under two seconds, reinforcing that this trial is worth starting now.

sections.name: Pricing — Trial Entry Point
sections.rationale: PricingCard is the core decision-stage conversion component for Movers. The Mover variant strips plan descriptions down to price and CTA only, removing deliberation friction. A single highlighted card for the recommended trial plan gives the Mover a clear, default choice and accelerates the signup decision.

sections.name: Final Conversion Banner
sections.rationale: CTABanner serves as the terminal action capture for Movers at the decision stage. The Mover variant uses a strong headline and a single CTA with no subhead — a last, unmistakable prompt to start the trial. Placed after pricing, it catches anyone who scrolled past the hero CTA and is now ready to act.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal's with-cta variant is essential on this decision-stage conversion page. It recaptures departing Mover users who scrolled to the bottom without converting, offering one final low-friction trial CTA alongside minimal legal and brand mark — no extra navigation to distract from the action.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Mover variant suppresses subhead and cta_secondary — one message, one action.

slots.headline: Design smarter. Ship faster. Start today.

page.reasoning: Mover variant is logos-only, no headline — silent credibility. logos-only is the highest-frequency placement.
Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact. 3-stat is the preferred default.
highlighted variant for recommended plan used at decision stage. Mover variant strips description; price and CTA only.
Mover variant: strong headline, single CTA, no subhead. Terminal action for converting trial users.

slots.headline: Your trial is one click away.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.
